description: ov run agentCount undercounts because the coordinator never increments the runs.agent_count column — only ov sling does. Symptom: run.agentCount = N but sessions WHERE run_id=? returns N+1 (coordinator + children). Root file: src/commands/coordinator.ts (createRun without follow-up increment); contrast src/commands/sling.ts:1100,1212 which does increment. Affects ov run, ov run list, ov run show header, and ov serve /api/runs.
resolution: Either (A) call runStore.incrementAgentCount(runId) after store.upsert(session) in both coordinator headless and tmux paths in src/commands/coordinator.ts, OR (B preferred) derive agentCount via SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE run_id=? at read time and drop the cached column. Option B matches the reporter's acceptance criterion and prevents future drift when sessions are removed or purged.

content: AgentState union split for spawn-per-turn substates (overstory-3087): the legacy 'working' state is the active rank for tmux/long-lived headless agents. Spawn-per-turn workers (builder/scout/reviewer/lead/merger under headless default) cycle through 'in_turn' (parser events streaming) and 'between_turns' (turn ended without terminal mail, waiting for next mail batch). Turn-runner writes the substates: first parser event transitions booting/between_turns/working → in_turn (src/agents/turn-runner.ts); turn end without terminal mail/abort settles to between_turns (not 'working'). SessionStore matrix accepts in_turn/between_turns as predecessors of completed/zombie/stalled, and CHECK constraint includes both. STATE_ORDER ranks them at 1 alongside working so a watchdog 'healthy=working' check does not stomp them. UI api.ts AgentState union and AgentTable.tsx STATE_VARIANT/STATE_LABEL render them distinctly; logging/theme.ts assigns colors/icons.

name: sqlite-check-constraint-rebuild-migration
description: SQLite CHECK constraint expansion pattern for in-place schema bumps: SQLite cannot ALTER an inline column CHECK, so to widen a state enum we (1) read sqlite_master.sql for the table to detect whether the new value already appears, (2) wrap a CREATE TABLE __new_<id> + INSERT … SELECT * FROM old + DROP + ALTER … RENAME in a single transaction, then (3) re-create indexes via the existing CREATE_INDEXES exec which is idempotent. Rows are copied verbatim — no in-flight remap is needed when the new values are additive. See migrateExpandStateCheck in src/sessions/store.ts for the implementation. Test: simulate the legacy CHECK by DROP+CREATE in a separate Database, then assert the new SessionStore opens and accepts the new values (src/sessions/store.test.ts 'migration: expand state CHECK').
